1A.6.note: All operations generated from OpenAPI via per-language generators

1B.2.note: Request/response types generated from OpenAPI schema

1B.4.note: Optional fields use pointers (Go), optional chaining (TS), nilable (Ruby)

1B.5.note: Date fields use time.Time (Go), Date (TS), Time (Ruby) per ISO 8601

1C.1.note: All API paths verified against upstream BC4 API documentation

1C.3.note: Paths constructed from OpenAPI spec via generated route tables; no manual path construction

2A.6.note: Error bodies truncated via Security.check_body_size! and Security.truncate

2D.5.note: Resilience patterns scoped per service.operation in Go; Ruby/TS use per-client config

3A.5.note: OAuth PKCE code exchange implemented in all three SDKs

3C.2.note: Response body size limited via Security::MAX_RESPONSE_BODY_BYTES

3C.3.note: Error messages truncated to 1024 chars via Security.truncate

3C.4.note: Authorization header redacted in all logging hooks

4B.5.note: AGENTS.md requires tests for every new operation

4C.4.note: Release workflows are idempotent; per-language workflows can be re-run independently

3C.6.note: Go SDK uses consumer-driven pagination; same-origin enforcement is caller responsibility

2B.1.note: TS retry middleware uses native fetch for retries, limiting chained retry count to 1; initial + 1 retry still provides resilience

2B.3.note: Ruby SDK only retries GET requests; PUT/DELETE sent once by design to avoid mutation side effects

2C.2.note: Ruby paginate returns Enumerator yielding items; X-Total-Count not exposed as metadata

1B.6.note: JavaScript Number loses precision on integers > 2^53; no clean fix without breaking SDK API surface

2C.4.note: Ruby paginate returns lazy Enumerator; maxItems cap requires ListResult refactor (separate PR)

2C.6.note: Ruby paginate Enumerator does not expose truncation metadata; requires ListResult refactor (separate PR)
